description: Quality gates for PRIME DIRECTIVE Phase 2 spec validation. Every spec must pass all gates to be considered reconstruction-complete. The self_improvement_rules section defines the MANDATORY three-loop autonomous system that any AI must execute: LOOP A (extraction regression), LOOP B (spec quality), LOOP C (meta-improvement).

L1_structural.description: Every IR field has a spec counterpart
checks: Every detailedVariable (except UberGraphFrame) appears in spec Variables section
Every detailedFunction (except ExecuteUbergraph_* and trivial UserConstructionScript) appears in spec Functions section
Every detailedComponent appears in spec Components section
Every implementedInterface is mentioned in spec
Every classDefaultValueDelta property appears in CDO Overrides section
Every dependencyClosure.moduleNames appears in Dependencies section
compilerIRFallback.hasUnsupportedNodes status noted in IR Notes
Identity section exists with Path, Parent, Generated Class (## Identity heading OR **Path:**/**Parent:** bold fields)
Purpose section exists
L1_structural.notes: validate_specs.py implements these checks. UserConstructionScript is excluded from mandatory L1 check — include in spec only when it has authored logic. Identity accepted as ## Identity heading OR **Path:** + **Parent:** bold fields.

L2_accuracy.description: Types, defaults, flags match the IR exactly
checks: Variable types match IR typeCategory + typeObjectPath
Replicated variables mention replication in spec
Network functions (Server/Client/Multicast) mention RPC type in spec
Override functions mention override status
Default values for key properties match IR

L3_logic_coverage.description: Every graph entry point and branch is represented in pseudo-code
checks: Every K2Node_Event in EventGraph has a corresponding section in spec Logic
Every K2Node_FunctionEntry for user functions has logic description
Branch conditions (K2Node_IfThenElse) are described with their conditions
Spec line count is proportional to graph complexity (not a 2-line summary of 50 exec edges)
Key function calls (meta.functionName) appear in pseudo-code

L4_reconstruction.description: An AI reading ONLY the spec can reconstruct a functionally equivalent Blueprint
checks: AI reconstruction produces matching variable declarations (count, names, types)
AI reconstruction produces matching function signatures
AI reconstruction produces equivalent logic flow
AI reconstruction produces matching component hierarchy
AI reconstruction identifies same C++ dependencies
L4_reconstruction.notes: L4 is AI-driven and expensive. Run selectively on high-priority specs or after L1-L3 pass.

L5_convergence.description: C++ from spec matches C++ from direct IR conversion
checks: Path A (IR → C++ converter) and Path B (IR → spec → AI C++) produce equivalent headers
Path A and Path B produce equivalent function implementations
Both paths reference the same #includes and module dependencies
L5_convergence.notes: L5 requires the C++ codegen tool (not yet built). This is the ultimate quality bar.

corpus_thresholds.notes: These thresholds gate whether a corpus-wide spec generation run is acceptable. Individual failures are logged to _VALIDATION_FAILURES.md for re-generation. LOOP B runs until ALL thresholds are met.

extraction_regression_gates.description: LOOP A gates — must all pass after any BlueprintSerializer plugin code change
extraction_regression_gates.stopping_condition: Suite exits with code 0 (suitePass=true in BP_SLZR_RegressionRun_*.json)
extraction_regression_gates.notes: Run after any non-trivial change to BlueprintSerializer C++ source. Build target is LyraEditor (never BlueprintSerializerEditor). Report written to Saved/BlueprintExports/BP_SLZR_RegressionRun_<timestamp>.json.

self_improvement_rules.description: MANDATORY THREE-LOOP AUTONOMOUS SYSTEM: Any AI receiving this context MUST run all applicable loops. The system is not optional — a spec corpus that has not passed validation has not fulfilled the PRIME DIRECTIVE.

loop_A_extraction_regression.trigger: After any change to BlueprintSerializer plugin C++ source code
steps: STEP A1 — REBUILD: Build LyraEditor (not the plugin name). Fix any compile errors.
STEP A3 — READ REPORT: Read Saved/BlueprintExports/BP_SLZR_RegressionRun_*.json. Check suitePass.
STEP A4 — FIX: If suitePass=false, read failures[], fix plugin C++, return to A1.
STEP A5 — COMMIT: git commit confirming all 14 gates pass in the message.

loop_B_spec_quality.trigger: After any spec generation run, OR at the start of any session where pass rates are below thresholds
loop_B_spec_quality.stopping_condition: L1 pass rate >= 95% AND L2 pass rate >= 90% AND L3 pass rate >= 85%
steps: STEP B1 — VALIDATE: Run validate_specs.py to produce _VALIDATION_FAILURES.md. Read the Summary section. If thresholds met, proceed to LOOP C then DONE.
STEP B2 — ANALYZE: Read _VALIDATION_FAILURES.md. Count unique error patterns. Note any pattern appearing 5+ times.
STEP B3 — UPDATE PLAYBOOK: For each pattern appearing 5+ times that lacks a CRITICAL RULE in ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md, add one. Commit: 'Playbook: add CRITICAL RULE for [pattern]'
STEP B4 — REGENERATE: Fix failing specs ordered by error count (highest first). Fully regenerate from JSON IR — do NOT spot-patch. Batch 10-20 specs per commit.
STEP B5 — COMMIT: git commit with pass rate delta in message: 'Spec: iteration N — M specs fixed (X% → Y%)'
STEP B6 — LOOP: Go to STEP B1.

loop_C_meta_improvement.trigger: After every complete LOOP B cycle (one pass through B1 → B6)
loop_C_meta_improvement.stopping_condition: No stopping condition — run after every B cycle. Final run after B reaches ≥95%.
loop_C_meta_improvement.purpose: Improve the process that improves the specs, not the specs themselves
steps: STEP C1 — GATHER EVIDENCE: Pass rate delta, playbook rules added, specs fixed, remaining failures.
STEP C2 — ANALYZE RULE QUALITY: Are rules precise? Root-cause focused? Do they have examples? Would a fresh AI follow them correctly?
STEP C3 — ANALYZE PROCESS QUALITY: Was full-regen used (not spot-patch)? Right BP order? Batch size appropriate?
STEP C4 — UPDATE ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md: Refine vague rules, add WRONG/RIGHT examples, fix rule ordering, add root-cause explanations.
STEP C5 — UPDATE ITERATION_LOOP.md: Improve protocol steps based on process analysis. Update 'Current State' section.
STEP C7 — TRIGGER LOOP B: Run next B cycle with the improved process.
meta_improvement_questions: Is the rule precise enough to be machine-verifiable (clear pass/fail test)?
Does the rule address root cause or just symptom?
Would a fresh AI reading only the playbook produce a passing spec?
Are rules ordered by impact (most-common failures covered first)?
Does each rule include a WRONG and RIGHT example?
Do any fixed specs still have errors (indicating spot-patching)?
Did new error categories appear from nowhere (may indicate validator bug or IR change)?
How long did the cycle take? Which steps were slowest?

playbook_update_protocol.when: Same failure type appears in 5+ specs (LOOP B STEP B3) OR rule quality analysis (LOOP C STEP C2) finds a gap
playbook_update_protocol.what: Add a new CRITICAL RULE to ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md describing: what triggers the error, the ROOT CAUSE (not just symptom), how to avoid it, what correct spec content looks like, with WRONG and RIGHT examples
playbook_update_protocol.commit_message_format: Playbook: add CRITICAL RULE for [error pattern]
playbook_update_protocol.goal: By iteration 3-5, ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md should be comprehensive enough that a fresh AI generates near-100% passing specs WITHOUT needing LOOP B at all

known_resolved_patterns.root_cause: Validator was treating UCS like any other function. UCS is auto-generated on all Actor-derived BPs and carries no authored logic in most cases — it should be optional, like ExecuteUbergraph_*.
known_resolved_patterns.resolution: Validator updated (v1.1) to exclude UserConstructionScript from L1 mandatory checks. ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md RULE 4 documents the include-only-if-authored-logic rule.

known_resolved_patterns.root_cause: Validator was checking for exact '## Identity' heading. Cursor-generated specs used bold **Path:**/**Parent:** fields at the top — equally valid identity info, just formatted differently.
known_resolved_patterns.resolution: Validator updated (v1.1) to accept **Path:** + **Parent:** bold-field format as equivalent to ## Identity heading. ANALYSIS_PLAYBOOK.md RULE 1 documents preferred ## Identity format.
known_resolved_patterns.impact: 135 false positives eliminated (some overlap with UCS pattern)

top_remaining_issues: Missing variables (bulk of failures — incomplete variable tables)
Missing CDO sections (BTS_CheckAmmo, BTS_SetFocus, BTS_Shoot, etc.)
Missing CDO properties (individual properties absent from CDO tables)
Missing user functions (non-trivial functions not listed)
Missing Logic sections (BPs with nodes but no ## Logic section)
current_status.next_action: Run LOOP B — start validate_specs.py, read _VALIDATION_FAILURES.md, fix specs ordered by error count. After each B cycle, run LOOP C to improve the process.
